untranslatable_reason: Signal requires: (1) Ranking stocks by prior-year returns into deciles (10th vs 90th percentile); (2) Calendar-aware entry/exit logic (first trading day of January, last trading day of January); (3) Annual rebalancing based on percentile thresholds. The allowed indicators lack: percentile/decile ranking functions, cross-sectional relative ranking, calendar date filtering, and annual rebalancing logic. The 'returns' indicator provided is likely single-instrument returns, not cross-sectional decile rankings. This is a portfolio-level ranking strategy requiring multi-asset comparison logic not expressible in the single-instrument condition schema.
translation_notes: This is a classic mean-reversion/contrarian factor strategy (January Effect / Year-End Loser/Winner effect). It requires: (a) cross-sectional ranking of all stocks by prior-year returns, (b) decile bucketing, (c) calendar-based entry (1st trading day of Jan) and exit (last trading day of Jan). Standard backtesting engines handle this via universes, ranking, and periodic rebalancing functions—not via per-instrument indicator conditions. Recommend implementing as a separate factor/ranking rule in the backtest engine rather than as condition-based logic.
